owner_intent: Freeze the response shape and counting rules for P0 question-dropoff analytics now that stepful decision events exist in shared flow infrastructure.

counting_rules.dropoff_definition: dropoff_sessions = questionnaire_view_sessions - question_answered_sessions for the same category and step.
invalid_rows_policy: Ignore rows that do not carry a valid category from the shared decision catalog.
Ignore rows that do not carry a valid positive integer step.
Do not fabricate step from route or query when the event payload is incomplete.

ranking_rules.top_item: Pick the highest dropoff_sessions item across all categories; break ties by higher dropoff_rate, then lower step number, then category key ascending.
ranking_rules.by_category: Return one top item per category using the same tie-break rules.
ranking_rules.empty_state: If no valid stepful data exists, keep status blocked and return null/empty collections rather than fake rows.

dashboard_rules: First-screen P0 analytics may surface question_dropoff_top and question_dropoff_by_category once status is live.
Do not compute question_dropoff from questionnaire_completed or result_view as a proxy.
Do not let compare or utility events participate in question_dropoff.
implementation_gates: Worker A may extend backend schemas and frontend API types only in line with this frozen shape.
Worker A must add regression coverage for repeated rows within one session and for invalid rows missing step.
Worker B/C may assume explicit source propagation matters for supporting breakdowns, but it is not part of the question_dropoff counting identity.
